pattern: Each query asks for the most frequent value in a range that clears a count threshold, breaking ties toward the smaller value, which a frequency tally over the range answers directly.
hints: For a single query you only care about how often each value occurs inside the given window.
Among values whose count reaches the threshold, you want the largest count, and when two counts tie you want the smaller value.

Tally the window's frequencies, then scan candidates from smallest value upward keeping the running best by strictly-greater frequency, so the smallest value wins any tie; return -1 if nothing meets the threshold.

edge_inputs.description: mix of met and unmet thresholds with a tie
single element window with threshold one
tie in frequency resolves to the smaller value
threshold exactly equals the top frequency
large values and a full-range query

stress.description: many short queries over a small-alphabet array so thresholds and ties trigger often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 8) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, size)
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        a = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        b = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if a > b:
            a, b = b, a
        th = rng.randint(1, b - a + 1)
        queries.append([a, b, th])
    return [nums, queries]
stress.note: small alphabet keeps frequencies high so thresholds bite
